outlook.headline: Premium demand holds, but low-end PC/smartphone demand stays pressured
outlook.summary: Mentions are still elevated at 4.4x, but the slight momentum fade and company reads point to a split consumer backdrop rather than a clean recovery. Premium smartphones, AI-driven content and select design wins are holding up, while low- and mid-end phones/PCs are being squeezed by higher memory and system prices; suppliers tied to flagship content should fare better than those exposed to mass-market units.

drivers.label: Premium smartphone resilience
drivers.detail: Flagship/premium handset demand is still healthy and showing improvement in some channels.

drivers.label: Memory and component inflation pressuring demand
drivers.detail: Higher memory prices and broader component cost inflation are reducing consumer affordability and weighing on unit demand.

drivers.label: Low-end smartphone and PC weakness
drivers.detail: Reads point to muted PC demand, challenged smartphone shipments, and weaker low-/mid-end consumer electronics demand.

drivers.label: Consumer AI content/design wins broadening
drivers.detail: AI adoption and new design wins are creating incremental demand pockets in PCs, smartphones and tablets.

risks: Memory pricing or availability tightens further, deepening consumer unit pressure.
Premium smartphone strength fails to offset ongoing PC and gaming softness.
High-single-digit smartphone shipment declines prove worse than expected.
